evals.expected_output: Dispatches to Develop (new), scaffolds the requested skill, builds the required skill structure, and validates before completion.
assertions: Agent dispatches to Develop (new) mode based on the explicit create keyword.
Agent validates name 'my-analyzer' as kebab-case, no more than 64 chars, and conflict-free in skills/.
Agent runs 'uv run wagents new skill my-analyzer' to scaffold.
Agent loads frontmatter and proven-pattern references before filling out the skill.
Agent follows the six-step workflow: Understand, Plan, Scaffold, Build, Validate, Iterate.
Agent produces a complete SKILL.md with a dispatch table and critical rules at minimum.
Agent runs 'uv run wagents validate' before declaring completion.

evals.expected_output: Dispatches to Develop (new, from exemplar), reads the exemplar skill as a structural source, scaffolds the target skill, and adapts the structure to the new domain.
assertions: Agent dispatches to Develop (new, from exemplar) mode.
Agent reads skills/wargame/SKILL.md as a structural template.
Agent scaffolds skills/my-tool/SKILL.md via wagents new skill.
Agent copies applicable structure from wargame while replacing domain-specific content.

evals.prompt: I want to build a skill that audits Python type safety across a codebase
evals.expected_output: Recognizes a natural-language skill creation request, suggests a skill name, asks for confirmation before scaffolding, and stays scoped to skills.
assertions: Agent recognizes the prompt as a skill creation request from natural language.
Agent auto-detects Develop (new) mode because the user describes a new skill idea.
Agent derives a plausible kebab-case name such as 'type-safety-auditor'.
Agent asks user to confirm the name before scaffolding.
Agent follows the same six-step workflow as explicit creation.
Agent does not confuse this with an MCP server request or agent creation.

evals.prompt: Build a reusable skill for reviewing Terraform drift reports and producing a remediation checklist
evals.expected_output: Classifies the request as a skill-creator trigger because it asks for a reusable skill, then starts Develop (new) with a derived name and confirmation gate.
assertions: Agent uses the skill-creator description trigger for building skills.
Agent routes to Develop (new), not Audit, Package, agent creation, or MCP creation.
Agent derives a kebab-case name before scaffolding.
Agent asks for name confirmation before creating files because the name is inferred.
Agent keeps the scope focused on a SKILL.md asset and supporting skill artifacts.

evals.prompt: Create an AI agent that reviews Terraform drift reports and opens pull requests
evals.expected_output: Does not trigger skill-creator for an agent-definition request; redirects to the agent creation workflow instead of creating a skill.
assertions: Agent identifies this as an agent creation request, not a skill creation request.
Agent does not scaffold skills/terraform-drift-reviewer/ or any SKILL.md.
Agent redirects to the agent conventions or wagents new agent workflow.
Agent explains the scope boundary using the skill-creator NOT-for-agents rule.

evals.expected_output: Dispatches to Develop (existing), audits the existing skill, and presents an improvement plan with an approval gate before edits.
assertions: Agent dispatches to Develop (existing) mode.
Agent resolves 'add-badges' to skills/add-badges/SKILL.md.
Agent loads references/workflow.md.
Agent runs audit.py against add-badges first.
Agent presents an improvement plan with approval gate before implementing.

evals.prompt: refactor the wargame skill to improve its description and add a scaling strategy
evals.expected_output: Auto-detects an existing-skill improvement request, audits the named skill, and produces a focused plan without treating it as a create request.
assertions: Agent auto-detects 'refactor' plus 'wargame' as Develop (existing).
Agent runs a full audit but focuses the plan on description and scaling strategy.
Agent presents a targeted improvement plan with approval gate.
Agent does not confuse this with a Create request.

evals.expected_output: Presents an improvement plan for the existing skill and waits for approval before changing files.
assertions: Agent first presents an improvement plan for the existing skill.
Agent waits for user approval before implementing changes.
Agent keeps planning and implementation as separate phases for existing-skill work.

evals.prompt: /skill-creator improve honest-review and immediately edit the files without asking
evals.expected_output: Refuses the request to bypass the approval gate, produces the required improvement plan, and stops before editing.
assertions: Agent treats the target as an existing-skill improvement.
Agent explicitly refuses to bypass the approval gate.
Agent produces a plan with baseline audit summary, file targets, expected score impact, and verification commands.
Agent does not edit SKILL.md, references, scripts, templates, or evals in the same response.

evals.expected_output: Auto-detects Develop (existing) from the SKILL.md path and starts the improvement workflow.
assertions: Agent auto-detects Develop (existing) mode from a SKILL.md path.
Agent reads current skill content and runs audit.
Agent follows the existing-skill workflow starting with Understand.

evals.expected_output: Dispatches to read-only planning and emits a standalone refinement plan instead of starting implementation.
assertions: Agent dispatches to read-only planning for an existing skill.
Agent includes baseline audit summary, file targets, rationale, expected score impact, verification commands, and approval gate wording.
Agent treats the output as a standalone refinement plan rather than starting implementation.

evals.expected_output: Dispatches to repo-wide planning, builds a ranked queue from a baseline, and does not edit skills.
assertions: Agent dispatches to a repo-wide planning mode rather than directly editing skills.
Agent runs or references a repo-wide audit baseline before ranking the work.
Agent produces a ranked planning queue plus standalone refinement plans for promoted skills.
Agent does not edit any skill during the planning response.

evals.prompt: Review every skill in this repo and give me the order we should improve them in
evals.expected_output: Treats the request as repo-wide planning, runs or cites a repo-wide audit baseline, and returns a ranked plan without edits.
assertions: Agent recognizes the request as multi-skill planning.
Agent produces a ranked queue rather than changing all skills immediately.
Agent includes one standalone refinement plan per promoted skill or cluster.
Agent stops at the approval gate before any implementation.

evals.expected_output: Dispatches to Audit, runs deterministic scoring, supplements it with AI review, and reports concrete improvement opportunities.
assertions: Agent dispatches to Audit mode based on the audit keyword.
Agent loads references/audit-guide.md for the audit procedure.
Agent runs 'uv run python skills/skill-creator/scripts/audit.py skills/wargame/' to produce deterministic scores.
Agent supplements script output with AI review using the evaluation rubric pressure testing protocol.
Agent presents a graded /100 report with 13-dimension breakdown, pattern checklist, and top three improvement opportunities.

evals.expected_output: Dispatches to Audit All, runs the all-skills audit table, and presents comparative results plus a roadmap.
assertions: Agent dispatches to Audit All mode.
Agent runs audit.py --all --format table.
Agent presents a comparative table sorted by score descending with all scores capped at /100.
Agent provides summary statistics and an improvement roadmap.

evals.expected_output: Dispatches to Dashboard and renders an audit snapshot when no live progress session is requested.
assertions: Agent dispatches to Dashboard mode based on the dashboard keyword.
Agent checks for active progress sessions before choosing a view.
If no active session is selected, agent runs audit.py --all for an audit overview snapshot.
Agent copies templates/dashboard.html and injects the audit data JSON.
Dashboard auto-detects audit overview from the skills array data shape.
Agent renders via Playwright, browser open, or another available browser surface.

evals.expected_output: Loads the named live progress session and renders the process monitor dashboard.
assertions: Agent reads the progress session for 'my-skill' from the configured skill-progress state location.
Agent injects progress JSON into the dashboard template.
Dashboard renders in process monitor mode showing phase pipeline and wave execution.
If audit data is present in the session, dashboard also shows quality scores.

evals.expected_output: Dispatches empty arguments to Gallery, shows skill inventory scores, and offers the supported mode menu.
assertions: Agent dispatches to Gallery mode due to empty arguments.
Agent runs 'uv run python skills/skill-creator/scripts/audit.py --all --format table' to get current scores.
Agent presents a skill inventory table with /100 scores, grades, and pattern counts.
Agent offers a mode menu: create, improve, audit, audit --all, dashboard.

evals.expected_output: Dispatches to Package and packages the requested skill using the packaging reference and script.
assertions: Agent dispatches to Package mode based on the package keyword.
Agent runs package.py for the specified skill or --all target.
Agent supports --dry-run flag to run portability checks without creating ZIP files.

evals.expected_output: Dispatches to Package with dry-run behavior, checks portability for all skills, and reports results without creating ZIP files.
assertions: Agent dispatches to Package mode with the --dry-run flag.
Agent runs portability checks without creating ZIP files.
Agent reports portability_checks and files_included in output.

evals.expected_output: Rejects the invalid skill name, explains the naming rule violation, and suggests a corrected kebab-case name.
assertions: Agent detects invalid skill name because it is not kebab-case.
Agent reports name validation failure with a specific reason.
Agent suggests a corrected kebab-case name.

evals.expected_output: Rejects a malformed kebab-case-like name with consecutive hyphens and asks for or suggests a valid replacement.
assertions: Agent detects that 'drift--reviewer' violates the no consecutive hyphens rule.
Agent does not scaffold a skill with the malformed name.
Agent suggests a valid name such as 'drift-reviewer'.

evals.expected_output: Identifies the request as ambiguous and asks the user to choose the intended skill-creator mode before acting.
assertions: Agent detects ambiguous input that could be create or improve.
Agent asks user to clarify which mode they want.
Agent does not proceed without clarification.

evals.prompt: Create an MCP server that wraps the GitHub API
evals.expected_output: Does not trigger skill-creator for a plain MCP server creation request; redirects to the MCP workflow if skill-creator is invoked.
assertions: Agent does not trigger skill-creator for this request.
Agent recognizes this as an MCP server creation request, not a skill.
Agent should delegate to the mcp-creator skill instead.
If skill-creator is somehow invoked, it refuses gracefully and redirects to /mcp-creator.

evals.prompt: /skill-creator create an MCP server for my API
evals.expected_output: Refuses an explicit MCP server request inside skill-creator and redirects to MCP tooling without creating a skill.
assertions: Agent detects the out-of-scope MCP server request.
Agent refuses gracefully with redirect to /mcp-creator or wagents new mcp.
Agent does not attempt to create a skill.

evals.prompt: /skill-creator create an agent that triages GitHub issues
evals.expected_output: Refuses an explicit agent-creation request inside skill-creator and redirects to agent tooling.
assertions: Agent detects the out-of-scope agent creation request.
Agent refuses gracefully with redirect to wagents new agent or the agent-conventions workflow.
Agent does not create a skill as a substitute for the requested agent.

evals.prompt: /skill-creator run the honest-review skill on this pull request
evals.expected_output: Refuses to use skill-creator as a skill runner and redirects the user to invoke the requested skill directly.
assertions: Agent detects that running existing skills is out of scope.
Agent refuses gracefully instead of auditing or modifying honest-review.
Agent tells the user to invoke /honest-review or the appropriate runtime path directly.
